name: WDC Edinburgh Observatory Yearbook Metadata API
provider: British Geological Survey / World Data Centre for Geomagnetism, Edinburgh
theme_canonical: observatory yearbook metadata; historical observatory reports; structured PDF/link index; literature-derived geomagnetic data
theme_raw: WDC /metadata/yearbooks; observatory yearbook links; BGS and external observatory annual reports
coverage: Global/International observatory yearbook metadata indexed by WDC Edinburgh; v108 API response covered 62 observatory IAGA codes and included BGS-hosted HTTPS PDFs plus external FTP/HTTP yearbook links
license: WDC/BGS metadata and linked-provider terms; BGS-hosted PDFs use BGS/NERC non-commercial/research and reproduction conditions; external links are provider-specific and require per-source review
formats: JSON metadata API; linked PDF/yearbook URLs, including HTTPS, HTTP and FTP links returned by the API
access: WDC API endpoint /metadata/yearbooks with optional observatory and date filters; response records include observatory_iaga_code, start/end date ranges, and link arrays
doi_or_citation: WDC for Geomagnetism Edinburgh /metadata/yearbooks service
time_span: API response observed from 1840 to 2014 overall in v108; individual observatory ranges vary, e.g. GRW from 1840, KEW from 1842, ESK from 1908, LER from 1923, HAD from 1957/1958, and some external series in the 1990s-2010s
notes: Accepted in v108 as a distinct structured metadata/API row because it indexes yearbook links for 62 observatories, broader than the BGS scanned yearbook archive row. The API returned 827 yearbook records and 444 unique links: 375 HTTPS, 31 HTTP, and 38 FTP in the v108 probe. HTTPS BGS YBpdf samples returned valid PDF content. Several external HTTP/FTP samples were not accepted as individually verified downloads due to timeout, certificate, refused connection, or 404 behavior; they remain API-exposed leads requiring source-by-source quality review. Refreshed in v109 after Geoscience Australia external-link audit: 106 WDC yearbook records pointed to 12 unique GA legacy GEOCAT URLs, but current GeoNetwork API checks showed this subset is not reliable yearbook evidence. Several IDs are 403/404; readable IDs 63522-63525 resolve to AUSGeoid98 data-file metadata rather than geomagnetic yearbooks. These GA links remain WDC-returned leads only and should not be treated as verified GA yearbook downloads. Refreshed in v110 after Ebre/Obsebre external-link audit: WDC /metadata/yearbooks exposes 22 EBR/LIV records pointing to old www.obsebre.es PDF bulletin/yearbook links for 2004-2014, but all tested old Obsebre PDF/page URLs were connection-refused. The new official Gencat Observatori de l'Ebre pages are alive and confirm Ebro/Livingston context, yet their data-catalog/bulletin links still route to the old refused domain. Therefore Obsebre links are retained only as WDC-returned leads, not verified yearbook downloads. Refreshed in v111 after INGV/Roma TNB external-link audit: WDC exposes six TNB annual-report links on old roma2.rm.ingv.it paths. Direct httpx requests to the old URLs failed certificate verification, but modern INGV paths at roma2.ingv.it/images/osservatori/annuariTNB/ yielded 13 lychee-verified TNB annual-report PDFs from 2003-2005 through 2017-2018. The old WDC links are therefore stale discovery leads, while the modern INGV PDF paths are accepted separately as a verified archive row. Refreshed in v112 after GSI/vldb.gsi.go.jp external-link audit: WDC exposes nine ESA/KNZ/MIZ records pointing to old GSI report pages for 2009-2011, but those old report index URLs now redirect to HTTPS 404 pages. Current GSI geomagnetic data download pages are alive and expose IAGA2002, raw, graph and tabular downloads through POST-driven forms; these are accepted separately as a verified GSI data-service row rather than treating old WDC report links as live yearbooks. Refreshed in v113 after ftp.nmh.ac.uk external-link audit: WDC exposes 175 records and 38 unique FTP PDF links on ftp.nmh.ac.uk/wdc/yearbooks, but lychee marks all FTP URLs unsupported and same-path HTTP/HTTPS probes were unreachable or unresolved. A partial modern HTTPS substitute exists on intermagnet.org/yearbooks for several file families; 25 PDF URLs covering Australia, Niemegk/Wingst, Poland, Denmark and Swedish SGU reports passed lychee. FTP links remain WDC discovery leads only; verified INTERMAGNET-hosted PDFs are accepted separately as a direct PDF file-set row. Refreshed in v114 after unresolved ftp.nmh family follow-up: SGO/Sodankyla FTP yearbook files have an official current HTTPS replacement at www.sgo.fi/pub_mag/Products/Yearbooks/ with a browsable directory and verified PDFs for 2004-2013. BCMT current portal and DATABANK remain alive, but same-name BCMT PDF replacements were not verified in this pass, so old ftp.nmh BCMT files remain deferred. Refreshed in v115 during BCMT unresolved FTP-family follow-up: old ftp.nmh BCMT PDF yearbooks are still not verified as direct PDFs, but current BCMT DOI-backed MAG.VAR and MAG.DEF databank collections provide strong file-level access for BCMT observatory data and are accepted as a separate row. Refreshed in v116 after VOLUME2014 follow-up: old ftp.nmh VOLUME2014.pdf links remain unsupported as PDFs, but the corresponding 2014 global INTERMAGNET definitive digital-data package is available through GFZ/DataCite DOI 10.5880/INTERMAGNET.2014 with a browsable download directory. This is accepted separately as a verified annual package row.
seen_in_passes: v108 WDC yearbook metadata API review; WDC OpenAPI -> /metadata/yearbooks -> filtered GRW/KEW/ESK-LER-HAD samples -> BGS HTTPS PDF range checks -> external HTTP/FTP caution checks; v109 GA external yearbook link audit; v110 Ebre/Obsebre external yearbook link audit; v111 INGV/Roma TNB external yearbook link audit; v112 GSI/vldb external yearbook and data-service audit; v113 ftp.nmh / INTERMAGNET hosted yearbooks audit; v114 SGO/BCMT unresolved ftp.nmh family follow-up; v115 BCMT DOI databank follow-up for unresolved ftp.nmh family; v116 VOLUME2014 GFZ INTERMAGNET annual package follow-up
desc_zh: WDC Edinburgh Observatory Yearbook Metadata API 是 WDC Edinburgh 提供的结构化年鉴索引服务，按 IAGA 台站代码返回年鉴记录的起止日期和链接数组。v108 抽查全量响应，API 覆盖 62 个台站、827 条 yearbook 记录和 444 个唯一链接，时间范围从 1840 年延伸到 2014 年；其中包括 BGS 托管的 HTTPS 扫描 PDF，也包括外部 HTTP/FTP 年鉴链接。BGS HTTPS PDF 样本可直接返回有效 PDF 内容，但外部 FTP/HTTP 样本存在超时、证书、连接拒绝或 404 等问题，因此这些外部链接只作为 WDC API 暴露的线索，需要逐源复核。该服务适合追踪历史观测台年鉴、文献型地磁观测记录和跨机构年报来源。 v110 对 Obsebre/Ebre 外部年鉴链路做了专项复核：WDC 返回 22 条 EBR/LIV 年鉴或 bulletin PDF 链接，主要覆盖 2004-2014，但这些 `www.obsebre.es` 旧域名链接在本轮 httpx、curl 和 lychee 交叉检查中均连接拒绝。新的 Gencat 官方页面可访问并确认 Ebro 与 Livingston 台站背景，但其数据目录和 bulletin 仍外链到旧域名，因此这些 Obsebre 链接只保留为线索。 v111 对 INGV/Roma 的 TNB 外部年鉴链路做了复核：WDC 返回 6 条旧 `roma2.rm.ingv.it` PDF 链接，但旧路径在 httpx 中证书验证失败；现代 `roma2.ingv.it/images/osservatori/annuariTNB/` 路径下 13 个 TNB 年报 PDF 通过 lychee 验证。 v112 对日本 GSI 的 `vldb.gsi.go.jp` 外部链路做了复核：WDC 返回 ESA、KNZ、MIZ 在 2009-2011 年的 9 条旧 report/index 页面，但这些页面当前跳转到 HTTPS 404。现行 GSI 数据下载菜单和表单页仍可访问，并通过 POST 生成 IAGA2002 ZIP 样本，因此旧 yearbook 链接只保留为线索，正向数据服务另行收录。 v113 复核 `ftp.nmh.ac.uk` 年鉴线索：WDC 暴露 175 条记录、38 个唯一 FTP PDF，但 lychee 全部标为 unsupported，HTTP/HTTPS 同路径也不可用或解析失败；同时 `intermagnet.org/yearbooks/` 下有部分现代 HTTPS PDF 替代，25 个澳大利亚、Niemegk/Wingst、Poland、Denmark 和 Swedish SGU 年报文件通过验证。 v114 进一步处理 FTP 年鉴未解家族：Sodankylä 的 `MagneticResults_Sodankyla` 文件在 SGO 官方 HTTPS Yearbooks 目录中找到 2004-2013 年可验证 PDF；BCMT 当前门户和 DATABANK 可访问，但旧 BCMT PDF 文件仍未找到同名可验证替代。 v115 继续处理 BCMT 旧 FTP 年报线索：旧 PDF 年鉴仍未转成可验证直接 PDF，但 BCMT 当前 `MAG.VAR` 与 `MAG.DEF` DOI 数据集合提供台站目录和文件级下载，因此作为 DOI-backed 数据集合另行收录。 v116 对 `VOLUME2014.pdf` 线索做了进一步替代核验：旧 FTP PDF 仍不可作为正向 URL，但 GFZ/DataCite 的 `10.5880/INTERMAGNET.2014` 提供 2014 年全球 INTERMAGNET definitive 数字数据包，可下载 829MB tar.gz、md5 和 readme。
desc_en: The WDC Edinburgh Observatory Yearbook Metadata API is a structured yearbook index from WDC Edinburgh, returning start/end date ranges and link arrays by IAGA observatory code. In v108, the full response covered 62 observatories, 827 yearbook records, and 444 unique links spanning 1840-2014; it includes BGS-hosted HTTPS scanned PDFs and external HTTP/FTP yearbook links. Representative BGS HTTPS PDF links returned valid PDF content, while sampled external FTP/HTTP links showed mixed quality such as timeouts, certificate failures, connection refusals, or 404 responses. The service is therefore valuable as a structured discovery layer for historical observatory yearbooks, literature-derived geomagnetic observations, and cross-institution annual-report sources, with external links requiring per-source validation. v110 specifically reviewed the Obsebre/Ebre external yearbook links: WDC returns 22 EBR/LIV yearbook or bulletin PDF links, mainly covering 2004-2014, but the old `www.obsebre.es` URLs were connection-refused in httpx, curl, and lychee cross-checks. The newer official Gencat pages are alive and confirm the Ebro and Livingston observatory context, but their data-catalog and bulletin links still point to the old refused domain, so these Obsebre links remain leads only. v111 reviewed the INGV/Roma TNB external yearbook links: WDC returns six old `roma2.rm.ingv.it` PDF links, but the old path failed certificate verification in httpx; 13 TNB annual-report PDFs under the modern `roma2.ingv.it/images/osservatori/annuariTNB/` path passed lychee verification. v112 reviewed Japan GSI `vldb.gsi.go.jp` external links: WDC returns nine old report/index pages for ESA, KNZ, and MIZ in 2009-2011, but those pages now resolve to HTTPS 404. Current GSI data-download menu and form pages are alive and a POST sample generated an IAGA2002 ZIP, so the old yearbook links remain leads only while the current data service is accepted separately. v113 reviewed the `ftp.nmh.ac.uk` yearbook leads: WDC exposes 175 records and 38 unique FTP PDFs, but lychee marked all FTP URLs unsupported and same-path HTTP/HTTPS probes were unreachable or unresolved; meanwhile a partial modern HTTPS substitute exists under `intermagnet.org/yearbooks/`, where 25 Australia, Niemegk/Wingst, Poland, Denmark and Swedish SGU report PDFs were verified. v114 further resolved part of the FTP yearbook backlog: the Sodankylä `MagneticResults_Sodankyla` files have verified 2004-2013 PDFs in the official SGO HTTPS Yearbooks directory; the current BCMT portal and DATABANK are alive, but same-name verified replacements for old BCMT PDF files were not found in this pass. v115 continues the BCMT old-FTP follow-up: old PDF yearbooks were still not converted into verified direct PDFs, but current BCMT `MAG.VAR` and `MAG.DEF` DOI-backed data collections provide station directories and file-level downloads and are accepted as a separate data-collection row. v116 further followed the `VOLUME2014.pdf` lead: the old FTP PDF remains unsuitable as a positive URL, but GFZ/DataCite DOI `10.5880/INTERMAGNET.2014` provides the 2014 global INTERMAGNET definitive digital-data package with an 829 MB tar.gz, md5sum and readme.
access_status: open API; BGS-hosted linked PDFs partly verified; external linked-file availability mixed, with GA legacy links partly stale/unrelated, Obsebre EBR/LIV links connection-refused, INGV/Roma TNB old links superseded by verified modern PDF paths, GSI old report links 404, and ftp.nmh.ac.uk FTP links unsupported after v113 audit
openness: open metadata / mixed linked-source terms; verified BGS-hosted PDFs non-commercial/research use; external links require per-source review and may be stale
source_type: official WDC metadata API / historical yearbook link index
evidence: https://wdcapi.bgs.ac.uk/metadata/yearbooks returned application/json with an observatories array. The v108 full response contained 62 observatory IAGA codes, 827 yearbook records, and 444 unique links spanning 1840-2014. Link schemes were 375 HTTPS, 31 HTTP, and 38 FTP; major hosts included geomag.bgs.ac.uk, ftp.nmh.ac.uk, obsebre.es, ga.gov.au, roma2.rm.ingv.it, and vldb.gsi.go.jp. Filtered calls for GRW and KEW returned annual start/end records with BGS YBpdf links; ESK/LER/HAD filtered calls returned current UK observatory yearbook metadata. Range GET checks for BGS HTTPS PDF samples such as YB_ABNGRW_1926.pdf, YB_ABNHAD1957.pdf, and YB_ABN_1927.pdf returned application/pdf content beginning with %PDF. FTP and some external HTTP samples were not counted as verified downloads because test requests timed out, had certificate/connectivity errors, or returned 404. In v109, the WDC yearbook records containing ga.gov.au links were isolated: 106 station/year records across Australian station codes mapped to 12 unique legacy GA GEOCAT URLs. The old URLs redirect to ecat.ga.gov.au GeoNetwork catalogue shells. Direct API checks found catno 40955 and 64810/65235 returning forbidden, 65560/66169/69264/70943/72697 returning resource_not_found, and 63522-63525 returning valid GeoNetwork JSON but for AUSGeoid98 v1.0 .dat data files with CC BY 4.0, not geomagnetic yearbooks. Therefore GA external links in the WDC yearbook API are documented as mixed/stale leads, not verified downloads. In v110, filtering WDC /metadata/yearbooks for Obsebre/Ebre/LIV found 22 records and 22 unique links: EBR ebre_2004_en.pdf through ebre_2013_en.pdf plus ebre_2014.pdf, and LIV livingston_2004.pdf through livingston_2014.pdf. All old www.obsebre.es PDF/page samples returned connection-refused locally and in lychee/curl checks. Current Gencat pages at /en/observacio/geomagnetisme/, /en/observacio/observacio-antartida/, and /en/observacio/ are alive and describe Ebro/Livingston observatory context, but their data-catalog, bulletin, and magnetogram links still point to the old refused Obsebre host. In v111, filtering WDC /metadata/yearbooks for roma2.rm.ingv.it found six TNB records: old links for Annuario 2001-2003 through 2008-2009. The old URLs failed with certificate verification errors in httpx. Modern INGV URL variants were then enumerated under https://roma2.ingv.it/images/osservatori/annuariTNB/. Lychee found 13 successful PDF URLs: Annuario_2003-2005.pdf, 2005-2006 through 2010-2011, 2012-2013 through 2016-2017, and Annuario_2017_2018.pdf. 2001-2003 and 2011-2012 variants were not accepted because they returned 404 in this pass. In v112, filtering WDC /metadata/yearbooks for vldb.gsi.go.jp found nine records for ESA, KNZ, and MIZ, pointing to old GSI report index pages for 2009, 2010, and 2011. httpx checks showed each old URL redirects from HTTP to HTTPS and returns HTTP 404 with a GSI not-found page. The current GSI menu at /sokuchi/geomag/menu_03/observatory_data-e.html returned 200 and listed Geodetic Observatories plus continuous observation stations and Fuji total-intensity stations with 1Hz, minutely, hourly, raw, graph, tabular, and IAGA2002 download form links. A session POST to datadl/download for KUJ hourly IAGA2002 January 2024 returned application/octet-stream attachment filename hour_iaga.zip containing KUJ202401IAGA.HOR. In v113, filtering WDC /metadata/yearbooks for ftp.nmh.ac.uk found 175 records and 38 unique FTP PDF URLs. Codes included AMS, BNG, CLF, DRV, MBO, PAF, PPT, TAN, KOU, CZT, PHU, LRV, SOD, TNB, QSB, BEL, HLP, HRN, NGK, WNG and others. Lychee marked all 38 FTP URLs unsupported. Same-path probes for https://ftp.nmh.ac.uk and http://ftp.nmh.ac.uk reported network unreachable; www.nmh.ac.uk did not resolve and wdc.nmh.ac.uk timed out. Modern HTTPS probes under https://intermagnet.org/yearbooks/ found 25 successful PDFs among enumerated candidates, including Australia_part1_2004.pdf, Australia_part2_2004.pdf, Australia_2006.pdf through Australia_2011.pdf, Niemegk_Wingst_2000.pdf, Niemegk_Wingst_2001_2003.pdf, Poland_2006.pdf through Poland_2014.pdf, Denmark_2000.pdf through Denmark_2003.pdf, sgu_cb23_8689.pdf and sgu_cb27_1999.pdf. The bare /yearbooks/ directory itself returns 404, so only direct PDF URLs are treated as verified files. In v114, the SGO magnetometer data page linked to Yearbooks at https://www.sgo.fi/pub_mag/Products/Yearbooks/. That directory returned 200 and listed MagneticResults_Sodankyla2004.pdf through MagneticResults_Sodankyla2013.pdf; direct PDF samples for 2004 and 2010 returned application/pdf and lychee verified the directory plus 2004-2010 PDFs. The BCMT homepage and /DATABANK/ were also checked alive: the homepage links Download, Plot, Realtime, Electric Data, INTERMAGNET and IPGP datacenter DOI records; /DATABANK/ presents DOI RE3DAT and CC BY-NC 4.0 conditions. However, old ftp.nmh BCMT PDF filenames still lack verified same-name HTTPS replacements after v113-v114. In v115, BCMT DOI landing pages and DataCite API records for MAG.VAR and MAG.DEF were verified, and file-level BCMT directories exposed direct IAGA-2002 and CDF downloads. This does not rescue the old ftp.nmh BCMT PDF filenames as verified yearbook PDFs, but it establishes current DOI-backed BCMT data access. In v116, VOLUME2014 was mapped to the current GFZ annual INTERMAGNET dataset: DOI 10.5880/INTERMAGNET.2014, landing page title Global magnetic observatory data 2014, and a datapub download directory with 2014_USB.tar.gz, md5sum and readme. DataCite describes the package as definitive digital values recorded during 2014 at INTERMAGNET observatories, including minute, hourly and daily vector values, baseline values, K indices and annual means.

provider: NOAA National Centers for Environmental Information

time_span: 1974-07 onward in the operational SEM archive; direct magnetometer examples verified for SMS-1 July 1974, GOES-1 January 1976, GOES-2 January 1978, and GOES-7 March 1987; later GOES coverage overlaps the GOES 8-15 science-quality row
notes: Accepted as the historical operational SEM magnetometer archive, distinct from the GOES 8-15 science-quality reprocessing row and from the SWPC near-real-time operational display/API row. The NCEI product page states that GOES 1-15 and SMS-1/2 satellites provided magnetometer, energetic-particle, and soft-X-ray data continuously since July 1974. Direct operational archive evidence in v126 confirms magneto CSV/NetCDF files in full-resolution and averaged directories for early SMS/GOES satellites. This row is intentionally broader and lower-level than the science-quality GOES 8-15 Level-2 row: it preserves the operational full/avg/plots access tree, including early GOES-1 through GOES-7 material that is outside the GOES 8-15 science-quality collection. Some month/satellite combinations are absent; for example, guessed GOES-1 1975/12 returned 404, while GOES-1 1976/01 exists. Use the product documentation and per-file metadata before scientific analysis.
desc_zh: NOAA NCEI 的 GOES 1-15 与 SMS Space Environment Monitor operational 归档保存了早期地球同步空间天气卫星的磁力计观测。目录提供 full-resolution 和 averaged 的 magneto CSV/NetCDF 文件，并配套质量控制 PDF 与卫星位置 NetCDF，可追溯到 1974 年 7 月 SMS-1，且包含 GOES-1、GOES-2、GOES-7 等 GOES 8 之前的磁场数据。它与 GOES 8-15 science-quality 再处理产品不同，更适合查找历史 operational 原始/平均产品和早期覆盖边界。
desc_en: NOAA NCEI's GOES 1-15 and SMS Space Environment Monitor operational archive preserves magnetometer observations from early geostationary space-weather satellites. The archive exposes full-resolution and averaged magneto CSV/NetCDF files, with quality-control PDFs and satellite-location NetCDF support, reaching back to SMS-1 in July 1974 and including pre-GOES-8 examples such as GOES-1, GOES-2, and GOES-7. It is distinct from the GOES 8-15 science-quality reprocessing product and is most useful for historical operational products and early coverage checks.
source_type: official operational satellite magnetometer archive
evidence: The NCEI GOES 1-15 Space Weather Instruments product page returned 200 and states that GOES 1-15 and SMS-1/2 satellites have provided magnetometer, energetic-particle, and soft-X-ray data continuously since July 1974; it lists MAGnetometer among the SEM instruments and identifies GOES-7 as carrying EPS, MAG, and XRS. The direct SEM archive root returned 200 and lists full/, avg/, plots/, sat_locations/, and science/. The full/ directory returned 200 and lists years from 1974 onward; avg/ and plots/ also list early years. SMS-1 July 1974 full/csv returned 200 and lists daily files such as s01_magneto_3s_19740701_19740701.csv. GOES-1 1976/01 returned 200 with csv/ and netcdf/ branches, while guessed GOES-1 1975/12 returned 404 and was excluded. GOES-2 1978/01 full/csv and full/netcdf returned 200 and list daily magneto_3s CSV/NetCDF files; avg/1978/01/goes02/netcdf returned 200 and lists g02_magneto_1m_3s_19780101_19780131.nc. GOES-7 1987/03 full/csv and full/netcdf returned 200 and list g07_magneto_3s daily CSV/NetCDF files; avg/1987/03/goes07 directories list magneto_1m, magneto_1m_3s, and magneto_5m CSV/NetCDF files. A representative GOES-7 daily NetCDF returned HEAD 200 application/x-netcdf with content-length 174,792 bytes; a representative GOES-7 daily CSV returned HEAD 200 text/csv with content-length 433,244 bytes, and a Range sample exposed metadata dimensions, orbit variables, time_tag, flags, and magnetometer data structure. GOES-7 plots/qc lists PDF files including g07_qc_mag_1m_3s and g07_qc_xrs_mag products. sat_locations/goes06 and goes07 list orbit-location NetCDF files and the GOES_6-15 ephemeris README.

time_span: BARREL payload-specific intervals during 2013-2016 campaigns; representative 1C SPASE record spans 2013-01-16 to 2013-01-26; registry valid-min/max metadata spans broader BARREL time bounds
notes: Accepted as a collection-level BARREL magnetometer row rather than one row per payload, because CDAWeb/SPDF expose dozens of homogeneous payload-specific L2_MAGN products. BARREL is a balloon mission rather than an orbital satellite or ground observatory; it is included because each payload carried a DC/tri-axial fluxgate magnetometer for ULF-timescale magnetic-field variations in auroral/polar near-surface regions, and the products are DOI-backed, publicly downloadable CDF files. The row is distinct from BARREL ephemeris, particle-count, housekeeping, and spectrometer products, which were used only as context or excluded from geomagnetic acceptance. Data are not gain-corrected or despun according to SPASE; users should follow the BARREL usage policy and quality flags.
desc_zh: BARREL 是 NASA Living with a Star 的气球任务，用于研究辐射带电子沉降，并在多个气球载荷上搭载三轴磁通门磁力计。SPDF/CDAWeb 为 BARREL MAGN Level-2 产品提供 SPASE/DOI 元数据、CDAWeb 查询和直接 HTTPS CDF 下载目录，典型采样间隔为 0.25 秒。该集合适合研究极区/极光区近地空间环境中的 ULF 时间尺度磁场变化，但数据未做 gain correction 或 despin，使用时需要遵循质量标记和 BARREL 数据使用政策。
desc_en: BARREL was a NASA Living with a Star balloon mission for studying radiation-belt electron precipitation, with tri-axial fluxgate magnetometers on many balloon payloads. NASA SPDF/CDAWeb provides SPASE and DOI metadata, CDAWeb access, and direct HTTPS CDF directories for BARREL MAGN Level-2 products, typically at 0.25-second cadence. The collection supports studies of ULF-timescale magnetic-field variations in near-surface auroral and polar regions, but the data are not gain-corrected or despun and should be used with quality flags and the BARREL data-use policy.
source_type: NASA SPDF balloon-borne magnetometer collection
evidence: CDAWeb registry NumericalData returned 200 and contains 44 unique BARREL *_L2_MAGN records, including BAR_1A through BAR_3G payload products with SPASE resource IDs and payload-specific DOI entries. The representative SPASE page for BARREL 1C Magnetometer L2 PT0.25S returned 200 and describes three-axis DC magnetometer data with nominal conversions applied, neither gain-corrected nor despun; it names the BARREL mission, notes that each balloon carried a DC magnetometer to measure ULF-timescale magnetic-field variations, identifies MagneticField measurement type, cadence PT0.25S, observed regions Earth.NearSurface.AuroralRegion/Mesosphere/PolarCap/Stratosphere, start 2013-01-16 and stop 2013-01-26, DOI 10.48322/8k18-dm67, FTPS/HTTPS from SPDF, and CDAWeb access. SPDF HTTPS root https://spdf.gsfc.nasa.gov/pub/data/barrel/ returned 200 and lists readme, campaign/launch files, and l2/. The l2/ directory returned 200 and listed 54 payload directories. The 1C, 2A, and 3F magn directories returned 200 and listed daily .cdf files. Representative file probes returned HEAD 200 application/x-cdf and CDF headers: 1C bar_1c_l2_magn_20130116_v10.cdf content-length 1,340,169 bytes; 2A bar_2a_l2_magn_20140116_v10.cdf content-length 2,756,715 bytes; 3F bar_3f_l2_magn_20150825_v10.cdf content-length 4,293,829 bytes. CDAWeb NotesB and eval2 product pages for BAR_1C_L2_MAGN returned 200. Campaigns_and_launches.txt and the BARREL data-use policy are linked from SPASE and support campaign/usage context. Ephemeris, housekeeping and particle/spectrometer BARREL products were excluded as non-magnetic-field rows.
access_class: SPASE DOI records + CDAWeb access + browsable SPDF HTTPS CDF directories
scale: 0.25-second Level-2 payload time series; daily CDF files; 44 unique CDAWeb BARREL L2_MAGN product records across many balloon payloads
feature_count: 44 unique CDAWeb BARREL L2_MAGN SPASE/product records; SPDF l2 directory lists 54 payload folders, not all necessarily MAGN products
file_size: Representative daily CDF files about 1.3 MB for BARREL 1C, 2.8 MB for 2A, and 4.3 MB for 3F; file sizes vary by payload and day

name: JHU/APL Freja F2 Magnetic Field Experiment 1 Hz Survey Data
provider: Johns Hopkins University Applied Physics Laboratory Space Department; Freja F2 Magnetic Field Experiment team; CNES CDPP-AMDA mirror metadata; Swedish Institute of Space Physics mission context
theme_canonical: Low-Earth-orbit auroral-zone magnetic field time series
theme_raw: Freja F2 1 Hz DC perturbation magnetic-field survey data and associated FFT/summary/orbit-characteristic products
coverage: Freja low-Earth auroral-zone orbit, 63 degree inclination and approximately 600 x 1700 km altitude, with 1 Hz survey files organized by orbit/date across the public JHU/APL archive
license: JHU/APL public web archive; contact/citation requested before scientific analysis or publication
formats: HTML landing pages, Apache directory listings, gzip-compressed 1Hz numeric data files, GIF quicklook plots, IDL reader routine, orbit-characteristic plots, SPASE metadata, AMDA HAPI CSV/JSON/binary service
access: JHU/APL Freja Magnetic Field Experiment web archive with direct /Freja/1Hz_Data/ directory listings and downloadable .1Hz.gz files; form pages support date/orbit lookup; CDPP-AMDA HAPI fja-mfe-1hz service provides an additional open query route
doi_or_citation: Freja F2 Magnetic Field Experiment web archive; data product description cites D.D. Wallis et al., Eccentric Dipole Coordinates For Magsat Data Presentation, Geophysical Research Letters, 9(4), 353-356, 1982, for coordinate reference
time_span: Freja mission magnetic-field archive pages expose selectable years 1992-1995; sampled file evidence includes December 1992 1 Hz records
notes: Accepted as one collection-level Freja F2 magnetometer row. The canonical URL is the 1Hz_Data directory because it exposes actual downloadable .1Hz.gz numeric data files; the project homepage, data-product description page, 1 Hz search form, FFT plot directory, summary-plot directory, and orbit-characteristic directory are retained as evidence rather than separate rows. The 1 Hz files are described as survey DC perturbation magnetic-field data after removing the Earth's main field and spacecraft spin motion, plotted in geomagnetic coordinates; the site asks users to contact L. J. Zanetti before scientific analyses or publications. The old hurlbut.jhuapl.edu Freja alias timed out and was not used as access evidence. In v160, CNES CDPP-AMDA fja-mfe-1hz was reviewed and treated as supplemental metadata/API evidence for the existing Freja collection rather than a duplicate row.
desc_zh: Freja 是瑞典低轨极光区卫星，F2 磁场实验由 JHU/APL 维护，其公开网页仍提供 1 Hz 分辨率磁场 survey 数据目录。该目录包含可下载的 gzip 压缩 `.1Hz` 数值文件、日期/轨道查询页、IDL 读取例程说明以及 FFT、summary plot 和轨道覆盖图等辅助产品。数据为去除地球主场和航天器自旋影响后的 DC 扰动磁场，适合先验浏览和极光区电流/扰动事件筛选；用于正式科学分析前应遵循网站的联系与引用要求。
desc_en: Freja was a Swedish low-Earth-orbit auroral-zone satellite, and its F2 Magnetic Field Experiment archive remains available from JHU/APL. The public archive exposes direct gzip-compressed `.1Hz` numeric data files, date/orbit lookup pages, an IDL reader reference, and supporting FFT, summary-plot, and orbit-characteristic products. The 1 Hz survey files contain DC perturbation magnetic-field data after removal of the main field and spacecraft spin motion, useful for browsing auroral-zone current systems and disturbance events; the archive requests contact before formal scientific analysis or publication.
source_type: institutional spacecraft magnetometer archive
evidence: JHU/APL Freja Magnetic Field Experiment homepage returned 200 and lists F2 1 Hz Data, F2 FFT plots, summary plots, and orbit-characteristic products. The data product description returned 200 and states that gzip-compressed 1 Hz DC perturbation magnetic-field survey data are available for downloading with a provided IDL routine. The /Freja/1Hz_Data/ directory returned 200 and exposes orbit/date subdirectories; /Freja/1Hz_Data/1000/ returned 200 with many `.1Hz.gz` files. A representative file, /Freja/1Hz_Data/1000/92-12-21_01_43_01001pn.1Hz.gz, returned 200 as application/x-gzip and decompressed to numeric magnetic-field records. In v160, AMDA HAPI info for fja-mfe-1hz returned startDate 1992-10-24T22:22:10Z, stopDate 1995-02-15T18:33:00Z, cadence PT1S, provider JHUPL, resourceID spase://CNES/NumericalData/CDPP-AMDA/FREJA/MFE/fja-mfe-1hz, and parameters fja_mfe_b and fja_mfe_pert. The SPASE page returned 200 with AccessRights Open, MeasurementType MagneticField, and ObservedRegion Earth.Magnetosphere. A sample HAPI data query for 1992-10-24 returned numeric magnetic-field and perturbation records.
scale: collection; orbit/date-organized Freja F2 1 Hz files and supporting plot products
feature_count: multiple orbit/date subdirectories with gzip-compressed 1 Hz data files; not exhaustively counted in v130
file_size: representative 1 Hz gzip file 52 KB; root directory listings and per-orbit file sizes vary

name: NASA SPDF/NSSDC HEOS 1 and HEOS 2 Fluxgate Magnetometer Recovered Archive
provider: NASA Space Physics Data Facility; National Space Science Data Center; HEOS 1/2 Fluxgate Magnetometer team; Imperial College data provider Peter C. Hedgecock
theme_canonical: Historical high-apogee spacecraft magnetic field archive
theme_raw: HEOS 1 and HEOS 2 fluxgate magnetometer hourly interplanetary magnetic-field vectors, single-point magnetic-field records, and reformatted recovered tape datasets
coverage: HEOS 1 and HEOS 2 high-apogee Earth-orbit / interplanetary-region measurements in GSE coordinates, with metadata intervals from 1968-12-11 to 1975-10-27 for hourly vectors and 1968-12-11 to 1975-12-31 / 1972-02-05 to 1974-08-02 for single-point products
license: NASA SPDF/NSSDC public data archive; recovered but unreviewed dataset caveat; citation/contact recommended through SPDF support and NSSDC user support
formats: HTML directory listings, NSSDC NMC metadata pages, text recovery warnings, TAR bundles, attributes.xml, .phys.1 recovered physical-record files, extracted .dat_ascii files, PDF data set documentation
access: SPDF/CDAWeb public HTTPS directory tree under /pub/data/heos/heos1/fluxgate_magnetometer/ and /pub/data/heos/heos2/fluxgate_magnetometer/ with downloadable recovered tape bundles and extracted files
doi_or_citation: NSSDC IDs include SPHE-00265 (Hourly Averaged Interplanetary Magnetic Field Vectors), SPHE-00644 (HEOS 1 Single Point Magnetic Field Data), SPHE-00001 (HEOS 2 Reformatted Single Point Magnetic Field Data), and SPME-00001 (HEOS 2 Single Point Magnetic Field Data)
time_span: 1968-12-11 to 1975-10-27 for HEOS 1/2 hourly interplanetary magnetic-field vectors; 1968-12-11 to 1975-12-31 for HEOS 1 single-point magnetic field; 1972-02-05 to 1974-08-02 for HEOS 2 single-point magnetic field records
notes: Accepted as a collection-level recovered archive rather than separate rows for each NSSDC tape/product because the SPDF directory tree groups HEOS 1 and HEOS 2 fluxgate-magnetometer products under one recovered offline archive lineage. Data quality caveat is important: SPDF states the recovered NSSDC offline datasets have not been reviewed in detail for sufficiency, description accuracy, or data quality, and may require substantial extraction effort. The archive is nevertheless accepted because it provides public, downloadable magnetic-field files, NMC metadata, recovered-tape bundles, attributes files, and documentation. Viking was reviewed separately in v131 but not added because the live SPDF Viking directory exposes hot-plasma, low-frequency-wave, and Viking lander particle products rather than a downloadable Viking spacecraft magnetometer time-series archive.
desc_zh: NASA SPDF 将 NSSDC 离线档案中恢复出的 HEOS 1/2 磁通门磁力计数据放到了公开 HTTPS 目录中，包括小时平均行星际磁场矢量、单点磁场记录和若干重格式化数据集。NSSDC 元数据说明这些记录包含 GSE 坐标中的三分量磁场、场强、方向角、航天器位置或单点 B-field 分量等信息，时间覆盖 HEOS 1/2 在 1968-1975 年间的高远地点轨道观测。该集合的价值在于补全早期航天器磁场观测，但 SPDF 明确标注为 recovered but unreviewed，使用者需要谨慎解析、核对文档并评估质量。
desc_en: NASA SPDF hosts recovered HEOS 1/2 fluxgate magnetometer datasets from the NSSDC offline archive in a public HTTPS directory tree. The collection includes hourly interplanetary magnetic-field vectors, single-point magnetic-field records, and reformatted recovered tape products, with NMC metadata describing GSE magnetic-field components, field magnitude, direction angles, spacecraft coordinates, and related record formats. It is valuable for early high-apogee spacecraft magnetic-field studies from 1968-1975, but SPDF explicitly marks the material as recovered and unreviewed, so users must parse the files carefully and independently assess documentation and quality.
source_type: NASA SPDF/NSSDC recovered spacecraft magnetometer archive
evidence: The SPDF HEOS root directory returned 200 and exposes heos1/ and heos2/ subdirectories. HEOS 1 and HEOS 2 fluxgate_magnetometer directories returned 200 and list hourly_averaged_mag_field, reformatted_hourly_interplanetary_magnetc_field_vectors, single_point_magfield, reformatted_single-point_magnetic_field, and single-point_magnetic_field products. The SPHE-00265 NMC page returned 200 and describes hourly averaged interplanetary magnetic-field vectors in GSE coordinates from 1968-12-11 to 1975-10-27 for HEOS 1/2, with old NSSDC IDs 68-109A-02A and 72-005A-01A and Imperial College data provider Peter C. Hedgecock. The recovered-data warning returned 200 and states that SPDF placed recovered NSSDC offline archives online but has not reviewed them in detail for sufficiency, description accuracy, or data quality. Product directories expose downloadable TAR bundles, attributes.xml, .phys.1 files, and extracted .dat_ascii files; representative tar, attributes.xml, and ASCII files returned partial-content responses containing recoverable archive/data content.
scale: collection; multiple HEOS 1/2 recovered NSSDC fluxgate-magnetometer products, directories, tape bundles, and extracted records
feature_count: multiple NSSDC dataset IDs and recovered files; v131 verified five product-level directories and representative downloadable files, not a full file census
file_size: representative product files range from kilobyte metadata/warnings to multi-megabyte TAR and physical-record files; examples include 2.1 MB, 40 MB, 42 MB, and 62 MB TAR bundles

name: NASA SPDF/NSSDC Azur Fluxgate Magnetometer Recovered Archive
provider: NASA Space Physics Data Facility; National Space Science Data Center; Azur/GRS-A Fluxgate Magnetometer team; Technische Universitat Braunschweig
theme_canonical: Historical low-Earth-orbit magnetospheric magnetic field archive
theme_raw: Azur biaxial fluxgate magnetometer 9.875-second averaged vector magnetic-field observations and 10-second magnetic-field-vector recovered tape products
coverage: Azur (GRS-A) low-Earth magnetospheric orbit; NMC metadata time span 1970-02-22 to 1970-07-09 with spacecraft latitude, longitude, geocentric radial distance, and magnetic-field components
license: NASA SPDF/NSSDC public data archive; recovered but unreviewed dataset caveat; citation/contact recommended through SPDF support and NSSDC user support
formats: HTML directory listings, NSSDC NMC metadata page, text recovery warning, TAR bundles, attributes.xml, .phys.1 recovered physical-record files, PDF data-set documentation
access: SPDF/CDAWeb public HTTPS directory tree under /pub/data/azur/fluxgate_magnetometer/ with downloadable 9.875-second and 10-second recovered tape products
doi_or_citation: NSSDC ID SPMS-00365; old NSSDC ID 69-097A-01A; Theile, B., NSSDC unpublished data set documentation for 69-097A-01A (GRS-A) - data format description, NASA-GSFC/NSSDC, Greenbelt, MD, July 1973
time_span: 1970-02-22 to 1970-07-09 according to SPMS-00365 NMC metadata
notes: Accepted as one recovered-archive collection covering the Azur fluxgate-magnetometer 9.875-second averaged vector observations and companion 10-second magnetic-field-vector TAR products. The SPMS-00365 metadata describes real-time measurements from a biaxial fluxgate magnetometer on binary magnetic tapes generated on a CDC 3800 computer, with logical records covering one 9.875-second subcommutated data frame. Important caveat: SPDF marks the recovered NSSDC offline dataset as not reviewed in detail for sufficiency, description accuracy, or data quality, and files may require substantial extraction effort. Transit-5E5 rubidium-vapor magnetometer was reviewed in v132 but deferred because the live package proves a tape image and dates but lacks enough accessible physical-variable documentation for a confident catalog row.
desc_zh: Azur（又称 GRS-A）是早期低轨磁层卫星，NASA SPDF/NSSDC 公开保存了其双轴磁通门磁力计恢复档案。NSSDC 元数据说明该数据集包含 9.875 秒平均的实时磁场观测，记录含日期、观测时刻、两个传感器方向的磁场分量、航天器纬度/经度、地心径向距离和太阳传感器输出；公开目录还提供 10 秒磁场矢量 TAR 包。该档案可用于补充 1970 年早期低轨磁层磁场观测，但属于 recovered but unreviewed 数据，正式使用前需要仔细解析二进制/恢复文件并进行质量核查。
desc_en: Azur, also known as GRS-A, was an early low-Earth-orbit magnetospheric satellite, and NASA SPDF/NSSDC hosts its recovered biaxial fluxgate magnetometer archive. The NSSDC metadata describes 9.875-second averaged real-time magnetic-field observations with records containing date, universal time, two sensor-axis magnetic-field components, spacecraft latitude and longitude, geocentric radial distance, and solar-sensor output; the public directory also exposes 10-second magnetic-field-vector TAR products. The archive is useful for early 1970 low-Earth-orbit magnetospheric magnetic-field studies, but it is explicitly recovered and unreviewed, so users need careful extraction and quality assessment before scientific use.
source_type: NASA SPDF/NSSDC recovered spacecraft magnetometer archive
evidence: SPDF /pub/data/azur/fluxgate_magnetometer/ returned 200 and lists 9pt875-sec_avg_vector_mag_field and 10-sec_mag_field_vectors products. The 9.875-second product directory returned 200 with AA_WARNING_recovered_but_unreviewed_dataset.txt, multiple DATA2_DR00405* directories, SPMS-00365_nmc.html, and PDF documentation. SPMS-00365_nmc.html returned 200 and describes 9.875-second averaged magnetic-field observations from the Azur biaxial fluxgate magnetometer, time span 1970-02-22 to 1970-07-09, old NSSDC ID 69-097A-01A, and Technische Universitat Braunschweig personnel. The recovered-data warning returned 200. A representative DATA2 directory returned 200 with a 79 MB TAR bundle, attributes.xml, and many .phys.1 files; a representative 10-second TAR request returned 206 application/x-tar with archive headers.
scale: collection; Azur fluxgate-magnetometer recovered NSSDC products, TAR bundles, physical-record files, and documentation
feature_count: two product families verified in v132: 9.875-second averaged vector magnetic-field observations and 10-second magnetic-field vectors; multiple DATA2 directories and TAR files present
file_size: representative 9.875-second DATA2 TAR 79 MB; 10-second TAR examples range from about 2.2 MB to 16 MB; metadata/warning files are kilobyte scale

name: NASA SPDF Hawkeye Magnetometer 1.9-Second CDF Data
provider: NASA Space Physics Data Facility; Hawkeye / Explorer 52 mission; University of Iowa / James A. Van Allen; Scott Boardsen metadata and despinning references
theme_canonical: Earth magnetosphere spacecraft magnetic field time series
theme_raw: Hawkeye HK_H0_MAG level-0 1.9-second despun magnetic-field data with GSM/SM vectors and spacecraft position
coverage: Earth near-surface ionosphere and magnetosphere observed by Hawkeye, with SPASE time span 1974-06-06 to 1978-04-28
license: NASA SPDF open data; SPASE metadata under CC0-1.0; cite Hawkeye MAG data and relevant despinning reference
formats: SPASE HTML/XML/JSON metadata, CDAWeb, HAPI, HTTPS/FTPS daily CDF files, mission README, documentation PNG/archived website, SHA1SUM manifests
access: SPASE landing page, CDAWeb/HAPI services, direct SPDF/CDAWeb HTTPS directories with daily CDF files, and mission 00readme/documentation directory
doi_or_citation: Van Allen, James A. (2025). Hawkeye Magnetometer 1.9-s Data [Data set]. Space Physics Data Facility; reference for despinning: Boardsen, S. and R. L. Kessel, Spin Index Determination of the Hawkeye Spacecraft, Journal of Spacecraft and Rockets, 39, 780-787, 2002
time_span: 1974-06-06 to 1978-04-28 according to SPASE temporal metadata
notes: Accepted as a modern SPDF/SPASE/CDAWeb row rather than a recovered-archive row. The SPASE record describes investigator-provided level-0 magnetic-field data at 1.9-second cadence with scalar field strength, vector magnetic field in Cartesian GSM and SM coordinates, spacecraft radial distance and position in GSM/SM, despin method indicator, and angular uncertainty estimate. Direct CDAWeb and SPDF directories expose daily CDF files for 1974-1978; a representative 1974 CDF returned valid CDF bytes via range request. The SPDF path shown in SPASE uses /hawkeye/h0/mag/, while the CDAWeb mirror also exposes /hawkeye/mag/mag_h0/; both were verified. Refreshed in v155 with the official SPDF Hawkeye 00readme: it identifies Hawkeye as Explorer 52, states launch/reentry dates, describes the highly elliptical polar orbit, explicitly lists tri-axial fluxgate magnetometer measurements with about 2 seconds between samples and daily files, and links the magnetometer directory plus supporting documents/plots.
desc_zh: Hawkeye（Explorer 52）磁力计数据由 NASA SPDF 通过 SPASE、CDAWeb、HAPI 和直接 CDF 目录公开提供，采样间隔约 1.9 到 2 秒。官方 SPDF 00readme 将其列为三轴 fluxgate/vector magnetometer 日文件，并说明任务在高椭圆极轨上观测近地电离层和磁层。数据包含标量磁场强度、GSM/SM 坐标三分量磁场、航天器位置、despin 方法标记和方向不确定度，适合研究 1974-1978 年高纬磁层与极区磁场结构。
desc_en: NASA SPDF provides Hawkeye (Explorer 52) magnetometer data through SPASE, CDAWeb, HAPI, and direct CDF directories at roughly 1.9 to 2 second cadence. The official SPDF 00readme identifies the collection as tri-axial fluxgate/vector magnetometer daily files from a highly elliptical polar-orbit mission observing the near-Earth ionosphere and magnetosphere. The records include scalar field strength, GSM/SM vector fields, spacecraft position, despin-method indicators, and angular uncertainty for 1974-1978 high-latitude magnetospheric studies.
source_type: NASA SPDF spacecraft magnetometer CDF collection
evidence: SPASE Hawkeye MAG PT1.9S page returned 200 and describes level-0 investigator-provided 1.9-second magnetic-field data with GSM/SM vectors, scalar field strength, spacecraft position, despin method, and uncertainty. SPASE JSON returned 200 and lists ResourceID spase://NASA/NumericalData/Hawkeye/MAG/PT1.9S, AlternateName HK_H0_MAG, cadence PT1.9S, observed regions Earth.NearSurface.Ionosphere and Earth.Magnetosphere, and time span 1974-06-06 to 1978-04-28. CDAWeb HAPI info for HK_H0_MAG returned 200 and lists B_total, B_gsm, B_sm, r, Pos_gsm, and Pos_sm parameters. CDAWeb/SPDF directories returned 200 for 1974 and 1975 daily CDF listings; representative hk_h0_mag_19740606_v01.cdf returned 206 application/x-cdf with CDF header bytes. V155 evidence: SPDF Hawkeye 00readme returned 200 and states that the archived datasets include tri-axial flux gate magnetometer measurements, vector magnetometer measurements with about 2 seconds between samples and daily files, the /hawkeye/mag/ access directory, documents directory, mission averaged magnetic-field plot, archived website, and Hawkeye master science file reference SPMS-00423; /hawkeye/mag/ and /hawkeye/mag/mag_h0/ directory probes returned 200.
scale: collection; daily CDF files across 1974-1978 plus CDAWeb/HAPI query services
feature_count: daily CDF files listed in yearly directories for 1974, 1975, 1976, 1977, and 1978; exact file count not exhaustively counted in v133
file_size: representative daily CDF files in 1974/1975 directories are commonly around 1.7-3.0 MB; SHA1SUM manifests are present

name: NASA SPDF Vega 1 and Vega 2 Magnetometer ASCII Archive
provider: NASA Space Physics Data Facility; Vega 1/2 magnetometer team; IZMIRAN reprocessed data; PI Willi Riedler; V. Petrov and V. Styazhkin contacts in readme
theme_canonical: Interplanetary and cometary magnetic field flyby archive
theme_raw: Vega 1 and Vega 2 fluxgate magnetometer cruise, Venus-to-Halley, hourly mean, and Halley flyby magnetic-field ASCII files
coverage: Vega 1 and Vega 2 cruise and post-Venus trajectory from 1984-1986, including Comet Halley closest-approach intervals on 1986-03-06 and 1986-03-09
license: NASA SPDF public data archive; use with citation to Vega magnetometer data providers and readme documentation
formats: SPDF HTTPS directory listing, README text, ASCII magnetic-field files, flyby 1-minute and high-resolution files, trajectory files, HGI/SE/CSE coordinate descriptions
access: Direct SPDF HTTPS directory under /pub/data/vega/mag/ with readme and downloadable ASCII files for Vega 1 and Vega 2 cruise and flyby magnetic-field data
doi_or_citation: SPDF Vega MAG readme, V. Petrov, Sep 2004; magnetometers PI Prof. Willi Riedler; data reprocessed in IZMIRAN, contact V. Styazhkin
time_span: 1984-1986 directory files; readme describes Earth-to-Venus cruise from 1984-12 to 1985-06, post-Venus to Comet Halley on 1986-03-06 and 1986-03-09, and sometimes later data
notes: Accepted as an interplanetary/cometary magnetic-field archive within the broader geomagnetism/space-magnetism atlas. It is not an Earth main-field product; it complements solar-wind, planetary, and cometary magnetic-field rows already present in the catalog. The readme states that each spacecraft carried fluxgate sensor units on a boom, describes data-rate modes, coordinate-system caveats for the one-axis cruise phase, SE/RTN/HGI/CSE coordinate definitions, and record layouts. A representative flyby_1m.vg1 file returned ASCII rows with BX_SE, BY_SE, BZ_SE, total B, and spacecraft CSE position columns.
desc_zh: Vega 1/2 磁力计档案由 NASA SPDF 公开托管，包含飞往金星、金星后至哈雷彗星以及哈雷飞越时段的磁场 ASCII 文件。readme 说明两艘航天器均搭载安装在 2 米吊杆上的磁通门传感器，提供 2.5 分钟平均、小时平均、1 分钟飞越和高分辨率飞越文件，并给出 SE、RTN、HGI 和 CSE 坐标及字段定义。该数据适合研究 1984-1986 年行星际磁场和哈雷彗星相互作用区磁场，但巡航期部分横向分量存在姿态方向未知的 caveat。
desc_en: NASA SPDF hosts the Vega 1/2 magnetometer archive as downloadable ASCII files covering cruise to Venus, the post-Venus leg to Comet Halley, and Halley flyby intervals. The readme states that each spacecraft carried fluxgate sensor units on a 2-m boom, and it documents 2.5-minute averages, hourly means, 1-minute flyby files, high-resolution flyby files, coordinate systems including SE, RTN, HGI, and CSE, and field layouts. The archive supports studies of interplanetary magnetic fields and the Halley comet interaction region from 1984-1986, with an important attitude caveat for some cruise-phase transverse components.
source_type: NASA SPDF spacecraft magnetometer ASCII archive
evidence: SPDF /pub/data/vega/mag/ returned 200 and lists 00readme.txt, flyby_1m.vg1/vg2, flyby_hr.vg1/vg2, trajectory files, annual ASCII files, and hourly-mean HGI files for Vega 1 and Vega 2. The readme returned 200 and describes fluxgate magnetometers on Vega 1/2, PI Willi Riedler, sensor arrangement, dynamic range and sensitivity, data-rate modes, cruise and post-Venus coordinate-system caveats, Halley flyby CSE coordinates, and record layouts. desc_en: The SESAME Fluxgate Magnetometer Key Parameters are Antarctic ground-based magnetic-field data served by NASA SPDF through SPASE, CDAWeb, HAPI, and daily CDF directories. The product contains H, D, and Z components of Earth's magnetic field as variations relative to an arbitrary baseline, with about 1 nT accuracy; the one-minute values are spot samples from the underlying 1 Hz sampling. The data support studies of Antarctic ionosphere-magnetosphere coupling and geomagnetic disturbances from 1993-2001, with the caveat that they are key parameters relative to an arbitrary baseline.

desc_zh: CANOPUS MARI Key Parameters 是加拿大 CANOPUS Magnetometer and Riometer Array 的一分钟本地极光电流指数产品，由 NASA SPDF 通过 SPASE、CDAWeb、HAPI 和 CDF 目录开放。该产品不是原始三分量磁场，而是由 CANOPUS 台站磁场扰动计算的本地 AE 指数上界 CU 和下界 CL，分别对应 AU/AL 的本地等价量。它适合研究 1992-1999 年加拿大极光区电流活动和磁扰动强度，但应作为派生活动指数使用。
desc_en: The CANOPUS MARI Key Parameters are one-minute local auroral electrojet index products from the Canadian CANOPUS Magnetometer and Riometer Array, served by NASA SPDF through SPASE, CDAWeb, HAPI, and CDF directories. The product is not a raw three-component magnetic-field time series; it provides CU and CL, local equivalents of AU and AL computed from magnetic-field perturbations at CANOPUS stations. It supports studies of auroral electrojet activity and geomagnetic disturbance intensity over Canada from 1992-1999, and should be treated as a derived activity-index dataset.

The old NSSDC documentation spreadsheet identifies `64-083C-01A / SPMS-00263 / 1964-83C` as “RUBIDIUM VAPOR MAGNETOMETER DATA,” and the public directory exposes a downloadable TAR package plus an NSSDC attribute file; the packaged data file begins with “SATELLITE 1964 83C MAGNETIC DATA APL/JHU.” This is a valuable open trace of 1964-1965 early satellite geomagnetic observations, but it is not a modern self-describing product, so column-level interpretation and quality control require additional documentation and careful decoding.

desc_zh: ATS 1 是早期同步高度磁层卫星，NASA SPDF/NSSDC 公开保存了其 UCLA 双轴磁通门磁力计恢复档案。该集合包括 SPMS-00544 的 15 秒平均矢量磁场数据和 SPMS-00545 的 2.5 分钟平均矢量磁场磁带产品；NMC 页面说明 15 秒数据包含时间、航天器状态描述符，以及 UCLA/ATS dipole VDH 坐标系中的笛卡尔磁场分量，时间覆盖 1966-12-07 至 1968-12-25。该档案对于早期地球同步轨道磁层研究很有价值，但属于 recovered but unreviewed 数据，使用前需要仔细解析磁带/物理记录并评估偏移和质量问题。
desc_en: ATS 1 was an early synchronous-altitude magnetospheric spacecraft, and NASA SPDF/NSSDC hosts recovered products from its UCLA biaxial fluxgate magnetometer. The collection covers the SPMS-00544 15-second averaged vector magnetic-field data and the SPMS-00545 2.5-minute averaged vector magnetic-field tape products; the NMC page states that the 15-second data contain time, spacecraft state descriptors, and Cartesian magnetic-field components in the UCLA/ATS dipole VDH coordinate system, with a time span from 1966-12-07 to 1968-12-25. The archive is valuable for early geosynchronous magnetospheric studies, but it is recovered and unreviewed, so users need careful tape/physical-record decoding and independent quality assessment.

notes: Accepted in v140 as a geophysics-relevant merged archive rather than a pure magnetometer-only product. The SPMS-00023 NMC page states that the data set contains merged data from all six Injun 1 experiments and explicitly includes Fluxgate Magnetometer data from a single-axis fluxgate intended to check magnetic-field alignment and determine detector look directions. The magnetometer range is listed as 0 to 5.E-5 T and pointed normal to the magnetic field vector. Important caveat: the mission did not achieve intended magnetic alignment because GREB 3/SOLRAD 3 did not separate, the combined spacecraft had complex spin-and-tumble motion, and the NMC page says loss of intended magnetic alignment rendered the data useless for detailed pitch-angle studies. Therefore this row should be used as an open historical source with magnetic-field context, not as a clean standalone vector magnetometer product.
desc_zh: Injun 1 `SPMS-00023` 是 NASA SPDF/NSSDC 公开的 Master File, All Detectors 恢复档案，时间覆盖 1961-06-30 至 1962-08-31。NMC 说明该集合合并了 Injun 1 六个实验，其中包括单轴 fluxgate magnetometer；该磁力计用于检查航天器磁场对准并确定各探测器视向，量程为 0 至 5.E-5 T，数据还包含时间、本地时、航天器位置、B、L、I、B/B0、Kp3 等参数。该条按“含磁力计的合并档案”收录，而非纯磁力计产品；由于 GREB 3/SOLRAD 3 未按计划分离导致姿态/磁对准复杂，使用前必须仔细参考 NSSDC 格式说明和质量 caveat。
desc_en: Injun 1 `SPMS-00023` is a NASA SPDF/NSSDC recovered Master File, All Detectors archive spanning 1961-06-30 to 1962-08-31. The NMC description states that it merges all six Injun 1 experiments and explicitly includes single-axis fluxgate magnetometer data used to check magnetic-field alignment and detector look directions; the listed range is 0 to 5.E-5 T, and additional parameters include time, local time, spacecraft position, B, L, I, B/B0, and Kp3. This row is cataloged as a merged archive containing magnetometer data, not as a clean standalone magnetometer product; because GREB 3/SOLRAD 3 did not separate as planned, the spacecraft attitude and magnetic alignment were problematic, so users need the NSSDC format notes and careful quality review.

evidence: The SPDF Injun root and Injun 1 directory returned 200; /pub/data/injun/injun1/merged/ returned 200 and lists AA_WARNING_recovered_but_unreviewed_dataset.txt, four DATA2 directories, SPMS-00023_nmc.html, documentation PDFs, and original scanned documents. The recovered-data warning returned 200. The old SPDF/NSSDC spreadsheet row 189 identifies 61-015B-05A, SPMS-00517, INJUN 1, MAGNETOMETER DATA.

desc_zh: Prognoz 10 是 Intershock 项目的航天器，NASA SPDF 公开保存了其 10 秒分辨率磁场 CDF 数据。样例 CDF 的全局元数据标明数据标题为 “Prognoz10 Magnetic Field”，逻辑源为 `prognoz10_10s_mgf`，仪器类型为空间磁场，变量包括 `Epoch`、GSE 坐标中的 `BX`、`BY`、`BZ`，单位为 nT。该条补充 1985 年弓激波、磁层顶和近 1 AU 行星际区域的历史航天器磁场观测，访问形式比恢复磁带档案更直接，适合用标准 CDF 工具读取。
desc_en: Prognoz 10 was an Intershock project spacecraft, and NASA SPDF hosts its 10-second magnetic-field data as CDF files. A representative CDF identifies the dataset as “Prognoz10 Magnetic Field,” with logical source `prognoz10_10s_mgf`, instrument type Magnetic Fields (space), and variables `Epoch`, `BX`, `BY`, and `BZ` in GSE coordinates with nT units. This entry adds historical 1985 spacecraft magnetic-field observations around bow-shock, magnetopause, and near-1-AU interplanetary regions, with a much cleaner CDF access path than the recovered tape-image archives.

notes: Accepted in v142 as the recovered Prognoz 9 triaxial fluxgate magnetometer product left for follow-up in v141. The NMC page gives a strong product description: 5-minute averages of magnetic-field vector data, spacecraft location in GSM and GSE coordinates, average magnetic-field components in each coordinate system, average field magnitude, directly computed average magnitude from high-resolution magnitude values, and GSE direction angles theta and phi. The DD079227/tap directory exposes the original .tap file plus 52 extracted .dat files and a tape-read report. Important caveat: SPDF marks this as recovered from NSSDC offline archives and not reviewed in detail for sufficiency, description accuracy, or data quality; files are binary fixed-block products rather than modern CDF.
desc_zh: Prognoz 9 `SPMS-00600` 是 NASA SPDF/NSSDC 公开的三轴磁通门磁力计 5 分钟平均 B 向量恢复数据。NMC 说明每条记录包含时间、GSM/GSE 坐标中的航天器位置、两个坐标系中的平均磁场分量、由平均分量得到的场强、由高分辨率场强直接平均得到的场强，以及 GSE 系中的 theta/phi 方向角；时间覆盖 1983-07-03 至 1984-02-07，共 52 个观测区间文件。该产品是重要的 Prognoz 9 历史磁场资料，但属于 recovered but unreviewed 二进制磁带提取数据，使用前需要谨慎解析格式并做质量检查。
desc_en: Prognoz 9 `SPMS-00600` is a NASA SPDF/NSSDC recovered triaxial fluxgate magnetometer product containing 5-minute averaged magnetic-field vectors. The NMC description states that each record provides time, spacecraft location in GSM and GSE coordinates, average magnetic-field vector components in both coordinate systems, field magnitude derived from averaged components, average magnitude directly computed from high-resolution magnitude values, and GSE theta/phi direction angles; the time span is 1983-07-03 to 1984-02-07 with 52 observation-interval files. This is an important historical Prognoz 9 magnetic-field source, but it is recovered and unreviewed binary tape-extracted data requiring careful format parsing and quality checks.

Feldstein. The NMC description states that records provide time, spacecraft location in GSM and GSE coordinates, average magnetic-field components in each coordinate system, average field magnitude, high-resolution-magnitude average, and GSE theta/phi direction angles, with 52 files and fixed 8,284-byte blocks of 4-byte real binary data. notes: Accepted in v143 as a directly downloadable full-resolution ASCII spacecraft magnetometer archive. OMNIWeb states that Prognoz 6 used an SG-70 fluxgate magnetometer from IZMIRAN with range +/-60 nT, accuracy about 0.3 nT, and one vector measurement every 10.24 seconds. The SPDF readme defines 17 fields: time, Btotal, Bx/By/Bz in GSE, By/Bz in GSM, and spacecraft position components in GSE/GSM. This row is distinct from the Prognoz 9 recovered 5-minute product and the Prognoz 10 CDF product already cataloged.
desc_zh: Prognoz 6 SG-70 磁通门磁力计数据是 NASA SPDF/OMNIWeb 提供的全分辨率开放 ASCII 归档，覆盖 1977-09-26 至 1978-01-22 的外磁层和太阳风观测。readme 说明仪器由 IZMIRAN 空间磁研究实验室提供，量程约 +/-60 nT、精度约 0.3 nT，每 10.24 秒给出一次矢量观测；日文件包含时间、Btotal、GSE/GSM 坐标下的磁场分量以及航天器位置。该数据可通过 SPDF 目录直接下载，也可通过 OMNIWeb Plus 浏览、绘图和子集化。
desc_en: The Prognoz 6 SG-70 fluxgate magnetometer archive is a NASA SPDF/OMNIWeb open full-resolution ASCII dataset covering outer-magnetosphere and solar-wind observations from 1977-09-26 to 1978-01-22. The SPDF readme states that the IZMIRAN instrument had a +/-60 nT range, about 0.3 nT accuracy, and one vector measurement every 10.24 seconds; daily files include time, Btotal, GSE/GSM magnetic-field components, and spacecraft position. Data are directly downloadable from the SPDF directory and can also be browsed, plotted, and subsetted through OMNIWeb Plus.

desc_zh: Prognoz 7 `SPMS-00383` 是 NASA SPDF/NSSDC 公开的三轴磁通门磁力计 5 分钟平均 B-field vector 恢复数据，覆盖 1978-11-10 至 1979-06-02。NMC 说明每条记录包含时间、GSM/GSE 坐标中的航天器位置、两个坐标系中的平均磁场分量、由平均分量得到的场强、由高分辨率场强直接平均得到的场强，以及 GSE 系 theta/phi 方向角。该产品与 Prognoz 7 全分辨率 ASCII 日文件不同，是二进制 recovered archive，使用前需要解析记录格式并做质量核查。
desc_en: Prognoz 7 `SPMS-00383` is a NASA SPDF/NSSDC recovered three-axis fluxgate magnetometer product containing 5-minute average B-field vectors for 1978-11-10 to 1979-06-02. The NMC description states that each record provides time, spacecraft location in GSM and GSE coordinates, average magnetic-field components in both coordinate systems, field magnitude derived from averaged components, average magnitude directly computed from high-resolution magnitude values, and GSE theta/phi direction angles. This is distinct from the full-resolution Prognoz 7 ASCII daily files and should be treated as recovered binary data requiring careful format parsing and quality checks.

Feldstein as contact. The NMC description states the product contains 5-minute averages of magnetic-field vector, spacecraft location in GSM/GSE coordinates, average components in both coordinate systems, magnitudes, and GSE theta/phi angles, written in binary on magnetic tape with fixed 27,000-byte blocks and 180-byte logical records. desc_en: Explorer 12 `SPMS-00648` is a NASA SPDF/NSSDC recovered fluxgate magnetometer product for 1961-08-16 to 1961-12-05. The NMC description says NSSDC generated it by merging Explorer 12 magnetic-field data with ephemeris information and elements of the 1961 Jensen-Cain geomagnetic field model; each logical record includes 10-second averaged magnetic-field quantities, time, orbit/location/McIlwain L ephemeris fields, and model-field values at 5-minute intervals. It is an important early magnetospheric field source, but it is recovered legacy data requiring format and quality checks.

desc_zh: Explorer 35 / Anchored IMP 2 / IMP-E GSFC 磁力计 82 秒 CDF 数据是 NASA SPDF 提供的历史航天器磁场开放归档，目录覆盖 1967-07-19 至 1972-02-23。抽样 CDF 元数据说明该产品由 SPDF 于 2010 年从 NSSDC `SPHE-00615` / `67-070A-04B` 转换而来，原始数据来自 NASA/GSFC 磁力计，包含 81.92 秒序列平均磁场、GSE/GSM/SSE 坐标下的位置或磁场相关变量、场强和标准差等。该行以 CDF 年/日文件为 canonical，同时记录 annual ASCII 文件作为等价访问形态。 v154 进一步核查了 `/pub/data/imp/imp2/fluxgate_magnetometer/82-sec_avg_mag_field/` 旧路径：NMC 明确该路径也是 `SPHE-00615` / `67-070A-04B` 的 Explorer 35 81.92 秒平均磁场产品，因此作为 legacy recovered-tape 访问证据补入，而不另立新条目。
desc_en: The Explorer 35 / Anchored IMP 2 / IMP-E GSFC magnetometer 82-second CDF archive is a NASA SPDF open historical spacecraft magnetic-field dataset spanning 1967-07-19 to 1972-02-23 in the public directory tree. A sampled CDF global attribute states that SPDF created the dataset in 2010 from NSSDC `SPHE-00615` / `67-070A-04B`, originally from the NASA/GSFC magnetometer, with averages over 81.92-second spacecraft sequences; variables include field magnitude, GSE/GSM magnetic-field components, standard deviations, and spacecraft position/support quantities. The CDF tree is treated as canonical here, with annual ASCII files noted as an alternate representation. In v154 the legacy `/pub/data/imp/imp2/fluxgate_magnetometer/82-sec_avg_mag_field/` path was reviewed; its NMC page identifies the same `SPHE-00615` / `67-070A-04B` Explorer 35 81.92-second product, so it is recorded as alternate recovered-tape evidence rather than a separate row.

The 82s_mag_cdf directory returned 200 and lists 1967-1972 year folders. Enumerating those folders found 1,394 CDF files from aimp2_mag_82sec_19670719_v01.cdf through aimp2_mag_82sec_19720223_v01.cdf. A representative CDF downloaded successfully; cdflib global attributes identify Project Explorer, Source_name aimp2/Anchored IMP 2/Explorer 35/IMP-E, Descriptor mag, Data_type 82sec, Logical_source aimp2_mag_82sec, Instrument_type Magnetic Fields (space), Time_resolution 82 second, and text describing creation at GSFC/SPDF in 2010 from NSSDC SPHE-00615 / 67-070A-04B. Sample CDF variables include Epoch, fldMAG, stdDevfldMAG, GSE_Bxyz, GSE_Bxyz_stDev, GSE_B_lat_azim, GSM_Byz, GSE_xyz, GSM_yz, and SSE_xyz. The 82s_mag_data_ascii directory returned 200 and lists aareadme.txt plus annual exp35_mag1967.txt through exp35_mag1972.txt files. v154 reviewed the legacy imp2/fluxgate_magnetometer/82-sec_avg_mag_field directory, which returned 200 and lists AA_WARNING_recovered_but_unreviewed_dataset.txt, DATA4_DR003861_DS003861_20100624_135117, SPHE-00615_nmc.html, and explorer35_mag_67-070a-04b_DSC_0214.pdf. The NMC page identifies title 82 s Averaged Vector Magnetic Field Data, NSSDC ID SPHE-00615, old ID 67-070A-04B, spacecraft Explorer 35, experiment Explorer 35: GSFC Magnetometer, time span 1967-07-19 to 1972-02-23, and says SPHE-00615 was generated at NSSDC from experimenter-supplied SPHE-00026 tapes by omitting the 5.12-second arrays while retaining 81.92-second averages. Sampled DATA4_DR003861 lists a 125 MB package, attributes.xml, and eight phys files; attributes identify transfer object SPHE-00615:1406320899:DS003861:1 and longest record lengths around 5526-5528.

desc_en: Explorer 33 `SPHE-00537` is a NASA SPDF/NSSDC recovered GSFC magnetometer product containing 5.12-second vector magnetic-field data from 1966-07-01 to 1968-10-05. It preserves the higher-resolution arrays that were not transferred to the 81.92-second average product `SPHE-00536`, but it is recovered binary tape data requiring careful decoding.

It is useful as the lower-resolution averaged companion product but remains recovered binary tape data.

desc_zh: `SPHE-00383` 是 NASA SPDF/NSSDC 公开的 IMP-C、Explorer 33/IMP-D、IMP-F 和 Explorer 35/IMP-E 多航天器小时平均行星际磁场历史恢复数据。NMC 说明该产品由这些任务的数据生成，目标是在 1965 年 6 月至 1968 年 12 月期间尽可能形成完整的一小时分辨率行星际磁场记录；每小时只由一个航天器覆盖，记录包含时间、轨道号、航天器标识和位置、小时平均磁场强度、GSE 纬度/经度角、笛卡尔分量及标准差。该产品是跨任务合成磁场记录，和 Explorer 33/35 Ames 小时均值 `SPHE-00344` 不同，但属于 recovered EBCDIC 磁带数据，需要谨慎解析和质控。
desc_en: `SPHE-00383` is a NASA SPDF/NSSDC recovered multi-spacecraft hourly averaged interplanetary magnetic-field product spanning IMP-C, Explorer 33/IMP-D, IMP-F, and Explorer 35/IMP-E. The NMC description says it was generated to make a nearly complete 1-hour-resolution interplanetary magnetic-field record for June 1965 through December 1968; each hour is covered by one spacecraft only, with records containing time, orbit number, spacecraft ID and location, hourly averaged field magnitude, GSE latitude/longitude angles, Cartesian components, and standard deviations. It is a cross-mission composite magnetic-field record distinct from the Explorer 33/35 Ames hourly product `SPHE-00344`, but it is recovered EBCDIC tape data requiring careful decoding and quality review.

The product includes GSE and GSM vector magnetic fields plus magnetic-field magnitude for studies of the magnetosheath, magnetosphere, magnetotail, radiation belt, ionosphere, and plasmasphere during 2004-2007. NASA SPDF also preserves a SwRI/MSSL IDFS mirror for the same mission with PRIME, SUMMARY, VEC and H/D/VIDF/PIDF files for users who need the original IDFS archive structure.

It supports studies of AC magnetic fluctuations in the near-Earth magnetosheath, magnetosphere, and magnetotail during 1984-1986.

desc_en: This dataset is the Nagoya University ISEE/ERG-SC 1-minute CDF archive for the Kototabang ground fluxgate magnetometer in Indonesia, with DOI `10.34515/DATA.GND-0024-0001-0101-1min-v01`. The ERG-SC directory provides daily `ktb` 1-minute CDF files by year; this phase verified 2003-2026 year folders and the sample file `isee_fluxgate_1min_ktb_20260101_v01.cdf`. The row is distinct from the same-station 1-second product and from the broader network entry, supporting lower-volume long time-series use. It adds an equatorial-region station product.
access_status: open public DOI landing page and HTTPS CDF directory
openness: open direct download with DOI citation and acknowledgement/rules-of-road requirements
source_type: official university/ERG Science Center station DOI dataset
evidence: The Nagoya DOI page returned 200 and identifies 1-min magnetic field data measured with a fluxgate magnetometer at Kototabang, Indonesia, DOI 10.34515/DATA.GND-0024-0001-0101-1min-v01. The ERG-SC 1min/ktb directory returned year folders from 2003 to 2026; the 2026 directory listed daily CDF files including isee_fluxgate_1min_ktb_20260101_v01.cdf (24 KB), and the sample file returned binary CDF content.
scale: single-station Kototabang 1-minute ground fluxgate magnetometer archive
feature_count: daily CDF granules by year; exact count not exhaustively counted in v167
file_size: sample daily CDF about 24 KB

name: Zenodo Solar Wind L1-to-Earth Propagation Delay Measurements from ACE and Ground Magnetometers
provider: Carsten Baumann; A. E. McCloskey; Zenodo; Journal of Space Weather and Space Climate supporting data
theme_raw: 380 interplanetary shock and sudden-impulse timing measurements based on ACE and ground-based magnetometer data
coverage: Earth magnetosphere response to ACE L1 solar-wind shocks; 380 events with ACE shock time and ground-magnetometer sudden impulse time
license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC-BY-4.0)
formats: Zenodo DOI landing page; TXT event-time table; Python pickle learning-set file
access: Zenodo open record with direct file downloads for measurement_SW_propagation.txt and final_learningset_SWdelay.pickle
doi_or_citation: DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.4300253; Baumann and McCloskey supporting data for solar-wind propagation-delay analysis, linked to Journal of Space Weather and Space Climate article DOI 10.1051/swsc/2021026
time_span: Event list starts in 1998 in sampled file preview; record describes 380 ACE interplanetary shocks paired with magnetospheric sudden impulses
notes: Accepted as a literature-linked derived geomagnetic event dataset rather than a raw observatory archive. The TXT file stores ACE shock times and SI times derived from ground-based magnetometer data; the pickle stores corresponding ACE variables for machine-learning propagation-delay analysis.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集提供 ACE L1 太阳风激波到地球磁层响应之间的传播延迟测量，核心文件 `measurement_SW_propagation.txt` 列出 380 个 ACE 激波时间和由地面磁力仪识别的 sudden impulse 时间。记录具有 DOI `10.5281/zenodo.4300253`，许可为 CC-BY-4.0，并附带包含 ACE 位置和速度等变量的 Python pickle 学习集。它不是原始台站磁场序列，而是基于地面磁力仪事件识别的可复现实证数据。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset provides L1-to-Earth solar-wind propagation-delay measurements linking ACE interplanetary shock times to magnetospheric sudden impulses identified from ground-based magnetometer data. The file `measurement_SW_propagation.txt` lists 380 ACE and SI event-time pairs, and the accompanying pickle file stores ACE position and velocity variables for machine-learning analysis. It is a citable, CC-BY-4.0 literature-linked derived geomagnetic event dataset rather than a raw observatory time series.
access_status: open Zenodo record with direct file downloads
source_type: Zenodo DOI repository dataset linked to peer-reviewed space-weather study
evidence: Zenodo API record 4300253 returned title, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.4300253, resource type Dataset, open access, CC-BY-4.0, and two files: measurement_SW_propagation.txt (15,596 bytes) and final_learningset_SWdelay.pickle (28,155 bytes). The TXT sample opened directly and showed ACE-time and SI-time columns with event timestamps.
scale: 380 shock/sudden-impulse event pairs plus ACE learning-set variables
feature_count: 380 event pairs described in record; two downloadable files
file_size: measurement text 15.6 KB; pickle 28.2 KB

name: Zenodo ULF Wave Radial Diffusion Coefficients from Ground-Based Magnetometers March 2015 Storm
provider: Louis G. Ozeke; Zenodo; ULF wave/radiation-belt study supporting data
theme_canonical: Literature-linked ULF wave geomagnetic derived dataset
theme_raw: Hourly radial diffusion coefficients derived from ground-based magnetometer measurements for the March 2015 geomagnetic storm
coverage: Earth outer radiation belt / geomagnetic storm interval 2015-03-16 to 2015-03-20; L* grid from 1 to 5 using TS05 magnetic-field model context
license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC-BY-4.0)
formats: Zenodo DOI landing page; ASCII DAT table; PNG plot
access: Zenodo open record with direct file downloads for DLL.dat and DLL.png
doi_or_citation: DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.1486292; supporting data for ULF wave radial diffusion coefficient analysis of the March 2015 geomagnetic storm
notes: Accepted as a derived geomagnetic/space-weather dataset because the record states the hourly DLL values were derived from ground-based magnetometer measurements during the March 2015 geomagnetic storm. It is not a raw magnetometer archive, but it is a citable open data product useful for ULF/radiation-belt coupling studies.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集提供 2015 年 3 月地磁暴期间由地面磁力仪测量推导的 ULF 波径向扩散系数 DLL。`DLL.dat` 是按小时给出的 ASCII 表，覆盖 2015-03-16 至 2015-03-20，并在 L*=1 到 L*=5 的 100 个网格点上给出数值。该条记录的是文献支撑的派生地磁/空间天气数据产品，具有 DOI `10.5281/zenodo.1486292` 和 CC-BY-4.0 许可。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset provides hourly ULF-wave radial diffusion coefficients, DLL, derived from ground-based magnetometer measurements during the March 2015 geomagnetic storm. `DLL.dat` is an ASCII table covering 2015-03-16 to 2015-03-20 on 100 L* grid points from L*=1 to L*=5, with an accompanying plot. The row records a citable, CC-BY-4.0 derived geomagnetic/space-weather product rather than raw magnetometer data.
access_status: open Zenodo record with direct file downloads
source_type: Zenodo DOI repository dataset linked to geomagnetic-storm ULF wave study
evidence: Zenodo API record 1486292 returned title, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.1486292, resource type Dataset, open access, CC-BY-4.0, and files DLL.dat (119,750 bytes) and DLL.png (64,863 bytes). The DAT sample opened directly and showed hourly DLL values on a 100-point L* grid for March 16-20, 2015.
scale: hourly radial diffusion coefficients on a 100-point L* grid for a five-day geomagnetic storm interval
feature_count: one DAT table plus one PNG plot
file_size: DLL.dat about 119.8 KB; PNG about 64.9 KB

name: Zenodo Australia GIC Index and Magnetometer Data Comparison Dataset 2024 Storms
provider: Colin Waters; Richard Marshall; Zenodo; Space Weather supporting data
theme_canonical: GIC and magnetometer storm comparison dataset
theme_raw: 60-second magnetometer data and modeled electric-field/GIC-index comparison files for May and October 2024 storms in Australia
coverage: Australian high-voltage transformer / magnetometer comparison context; storm intervals 2024-05-10 to 2024-05-11 and 2024-10-11 with station/file codes including CAN, CHT, HBT
license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC-BY-4.0)
formats: Zenodo DOI landing page; ASCII TXT data files; Python helper script
access: Zenodo open record with direct file downloads for B_GIC and Emodel text files
doi_or_citation: DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.16809945; Waters and Marshall supporting data for Space Weather 2025 GIC index and magnetometer comparison study
time_span: 2024-05-10/11 and 2024-10-11 storm intervals sampled at 60 seconds
notes: Accepted as a literature-linked GIC/magnetometer comparison dataset. The record explicitly describes 60-second sampled magnetometer data for May 2024 and October 2024 storm intervals and includes text files with Bx/By and GICx/GICy columns plus modeled electric-field files.
desc_zh: 该 Zenodo 数据集用于比较澳大利亚高压变压器实测 GIC、磁力仪数据推导的 GIC index 以及建模电场。记录说明包含 2024 年 5 月 10-11 日和 2024 年 10 月 11 日两个地磁暴时段的 60 秒采样磁力仪数据；样例 `20241011_CAN_B_GIC.txt` 可直接打开，表头包含 Bx、By、GICx、GICy。该数据集具有 DOI `10.5281/zenodo.16809945` 和 CC-BY-4.0 许可。
desc_en: This Zenodo dataset supports comparison of measured Australian transformer GIC, a magnetometer-derived GIC index, and modeled electric fields. The record describes 60-second magnetometer data for the 10-11 May 2024 and 11 October 2024 storm intervals; the sample file `20241011_CAN_B_GIC.txt` opens directly and contains Bx, By, GICx, and GICy columns. The dataset is citable via DOI `10.5281/zenodo.16809945` and is released under CC-BY-4.0.
access_status: open Zenodo record with direct file downloads
source_type: Zenodo DOI repository dataset linked to Space Weather GIC/magnetometer study
evidence: Zenodo API record 16809945 returned title, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.16809945, resource type Dataset, open access, CC-BY-4.0, and multiple TXT files plus a Python helper script. Direct samples opened: 20241011_CAN_B_GIC.txt showed YY MM DD HH Mi Bx By GICx GICy columns, and 20240511_Emodel_HBT.txt showed modeled electric-field columns.
scale: storm-interval 60-second magnetometer/GIC comparison files for Australian stations and transformers
feature_count: multiple TXT files for B/GIC and electric-field model intervals plus one helper script
file_size: sample B_GIC text files about 73.5 KB; Emodel text files about 200.3 KB

name: EarthScope IRIS SPUD Electromagnetic Transfer Function Database
provider: EarthScope / NSF SAGE Data Services; IRIS DMC SPUD; USGS Geomagnetism Program; Oregon State University; international magnetotelluric contributors
theme_raw: Electromagnetic / magnetotelluric transfer functions derived from natural electric and magnetic field observations
coverage: Global and regional MT surveys in SPUD EMTF, including USArray, USMTArray, USGS-GEOMAG, USGS-GGGSC, INTERMAGNET-related and other contributor projects; v169 service probe counted 8,210 EMTF item ids and 1,111 USArray project ids
license: Mixed per-item release conditions and citation requirements; SPUD product page cites database DOI and item/survey-specific citation panels
formats: SPUD web query; Spudservice XML; EDI download; ZIP bundle containing XML metadata and primary/attachment data; item DOI records
access: EarthScope/IRIS SPUD EMTF product page and Spudservice endpoints for ids, item XML, and bundles
doi_or_citation: Database DOI: 10.17611/DP/EMTF.1; example item DOI: 10.17611/DP/22066852; cite item/survey-specific Citation Info panels where applicable
time_span: Varies by survey and item; sample USArray item USArray.TTI55.2017 has product-id year 2017; database contains historical and modern MT transfer-function submissions
notes: Accepted as a database-level access backbone for magnetotelluric transfer functions. Although MT is not a raw geomagnetic observatory time series, EMTF records are derived from natural electric and magnetic field observations and are important for geoelectric, induction, and space-weather hazard studies. Single station/item records should not be added as ordinary rows unless a specific DOI-level subset is needed later.
desc_zh: EarthScope/IRIS SPUD EMTF 是电磁/大地电磁传递函数数据库，收录由自然电场和磁场观测推导的阻抗、tipper 等传递函数。v169 通过 Spudservice 直接验证了全库 `emtf/ids` 可返回 8210 个 EMTF item id，USArray 查询可返回 1111 个 id，并打开了样例 `USArray.TTI55.2017` 的 XML 和 ZIP bundle。该条作为数据库级入口记录，适合补充地磁感应、地电场和空间天气风险研究中常用的开放 MT 派生数据。
desc_en: EarthScope/IRIS SPUD EMTF is a repository of electromagnetic and magnetotelluric transfer functions derived from natural electric and magnetic field observations. In v169 the Spudservice `emtf/ids` endpoint returned 8,210 EMTF item ids, the USArray query returned 1,111 ids, and the sample `USArray.TTI55.2017` item XML and ZIP bundle were opened directly. The row records the database-level access route for MT-derived products relevant to induction, geoelectric-field, and space-weather hazard studies.
access_status: open web query and programmatic Spudservice access; per-item conditions/citation requirements may vary
openness: open searchable repository with downloadable XML/EDI/bundle products and item DOIs; observe per-item release and citation conditions
source_type: official EarthScope/IRIS data-service product and SPUD repository
evidence: Product page https://ds.iris.edu/ds/products/emtf/ returned EMTF metadata and database citation DOI 10.17611/DP/EMTF.1. The SPUD EMTF page documents downloads and citation panels. Spudservice /emtf/ids returned 8,210 ids; /emtf/ids?project=USArray returned 1,111 ids. Sample item https://ds.iris.edu/spudservice/emtf/22066852 returned XML for ProductId USArray.TTI55.2017 and DOI 10.17611/DP/22066852. https://ds.iris.edu/spudservice/bundle?22066852 returned an application/zip bundle beginning with ZIP magic bytes and containing XML plus data attachments.
scale: 8,210 EMTF item ids in v169 service probe; 1,111 USArray ids in project probe
feature_count: database-level repository; item-level count 8,210 from /emtf/ids probe
file_size: sample item ZIP bundle about 137.6 KB in v169 probe; database total not enumerated by size
